title: OOP
content:
Object-oriented programming is way of programming oriented towards the objects. An object oriented program is basically a collection of objects and relationship and communication among them. Related data and code that operate on that data is considered as object. Four main concept of OOPs are abstraction, encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism which help us to write modular, reusable, extensible and generic software system.

A class is a blueprint which defines the attributes (fields) and behaviours (methods) common to a number of objects. A class is a user defined blueprint based on which objects are created. Class is the unit of modularization in object oriented program. A class is defined using class keyword. A class body contains contains specification of a set of fields which defines attributes of the objects and a set of methods which defines behaviours of the objects. Defines attributes and behaviours of objects. Behaviours are used to change states. State is combination of attributes values.

Object is a concrete entity with a set of attributes and a set of behaviours. Object also have identify, lifetime and scope and belongs to a class. Fields defines the attributes of objects instantiated from a class. Methods defines the behaviors of objects instantiated from a class. Objects maintain state in instance variables (attributes). Objects are allocated from the heap and accessed by reference variables. Objects communicates with one another by sending and receiving message.

Encapsulation:
Encapsulation is the process of wrapping of related data and code into a single unit which modularizes our software system. Encapsulation also help us to achieve data hiding which is the process of hiding sensitive data of the objects from the outside world. Encapsulation is java is achieved through class. Encapsulation makes out software system modular ie. we can change one part of the code without affecting other parts of code.
Advantages of encapsulation:
  • Program is well organized so easy to maintain.
  • Data security: help us to make fields of a class read-only or write-only.
  • Encapsulation gives a clear and modular structure to our software system.

Inheritance:
Inheritance is a mechanism by which one class acquires the attributes (fields) and functionalities (methods) of another existing class. Inheritance enable us to create modules that does not need to be changed when new attributes or functionalities is added to the class. Inheritance helps to create reusable and extensible software components. Reusability helps us to avoid duplicate code and data. Inheritance helps us to define relationship among classes.
Types of inheritance:
  • Single inheritance - when a class inherits another class.
  • Multi-level inheritance - when a class inherits derived class.
  • Hierarchical inheritance - when a class is inherited by many classes.

Abstraction:
Abstraction is a mechanism of hiding unnecessary details and showing only essential details to the user by separating interface from its implementation. Abstraction also generalises by capturing the common essense among unrelated classes. Main goal of the abstraction it to reduce the complexity of the design and implementation process of software system. Abstraction can be achieved through interface and abstract class.

Abstract class is one of two ways to achieve abstraction in Java. Abstract class declared with abstract keyword. Abstract class cannot be instanciated. Abstract class cannot be final. Abstract class always have a default constructor. Abstract class can have parameterized constructors. Abstract class is created to be extended or inherited. Abstract class can have abstract and non-abstract methods. A class that extends an abstract class must implement all its abstract method. Abstract method is a method that is declared without implementation. Abstract method must always be redefined in the subclass. Abstract method only have signature. Abstract method can not be final. Class which contains abstract method must be declared abstract.

Interface:
Interface is one of two ways to achieve abstraction in Java. Interface is completely abstract class. Interface methods are by default abstract and public. Interface variables are by default public, static and final. Interface specifies only what needs to be done. Childrens will decide how to do in their own way. Like abstract class interface cannot be instanciated. Interface allows us to group several related classes as siblings. Interface defines what set of functionality classes that implements them will have.
Abstraction: Complete abstraction can be achieved through interface.
Security: Hides implementation details and only show the important details of an object.
Multiple inheritance: Multiple inheritance can be achieved through interface because a class can implements multiple interfaces.

Polymorphism:
Polymorphism refers to one-to-many relationship, which allows an entity such as an variable, a method or an object to have more than one form. Polymorphism enables us to deal with generalities and let the compiler and runtime environment to handle the specific. Polymorphism in Java can be achieved through method overloading and method overriding.
Examples:
  • Many methods can have one common name.
  • One method can accept many types of arguments.
  • One method header can be associated with many method body.
  • One type of reference variable can be used to access many type of objects.

Method overloading:
The process of writing multiple methods with the same name in the same class is called method overloading. Method overloading is a kind of static polymorphism.
Advantages:
  • Method overloading increases the readability of the program.
  • Avoids the need to memorize multiple method names.
  • Method overloading makes our code more generic.
  • Method overloading support clean code.
  • The complexity of the code is reduced.
  • Binding process happens during compile time.
  • Compiler binds the method call with an approriate method body.
Method overloading is achieved by either:
  • Changing the number of arguments.
  • Changing the data type of the arguments.

Method overriding:
Process of redefining a parent class instance method into its child class is known as method overriding. An instance method defined in the parent class is redefined into its child class to give specific implementation of the parent class method. Referenced object type, not the reference variable type determines which method will be called. When we create a parent class reference variable and assign a child class object then calling an overridden method on that variable will call the child class behaviour.

Facts about MO:
  • Final methods can not be overridden.
  • Static methods can not be overridden.
  • Private methods can not be overridden.
  • Child class method is called overriding method.
  • Parent class method is called overridden method.
  • Overridden methods are dynamically dispatched.
  • Finalize method can be overridden to free system resources.
  • Overridden parent class method can be called using super keyword.
  • Runtime polymorphism can be achieved through method overriding.
  • Abstract methods can only be overridden by the first concrete class.
  • The access level cannot be more restrictive than the overridden method.
  • Override annotation denotes that the method overrides the base class method.

title: Design Patterns
content:
Design patterns are reusable solutions to commonly occurring problems in software design. They provide a template for solving design issues in various contexts, helping developers write code that is more maintainable, scalable and understandable.
Design patterns are divided into three categories:
  • Creational patterns - Deals with object creation.
  • Structural patterns - Deals with object composition.
  • Behavioral Patterns - Deals with object interaction and responsibility.
Reusability: Design patterns provide a reusable solution to common software design problems. This saves developers from reinventing the wheel whenever they encounter a new problem. Instead, they can use a tried and tested pattern to solve the problem.

Maintainability: Design patterns promote clean, organized and maintainable code. This makes it easier for developers to maintain and modify the code as the software evolves.

Rapid Development: Design patterns provide a standard way of solving common software design problems. This saves time and effort in the desing and development process, allowing developers to focus on other aspects of the software.

Creational Patterns: Singleton, Builder, Factory Method, Abstract Factory and Prototype.
Structural Patterns: Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade, Flyweight and Proxy.
Behavioral Patterns: CoR, Command, Iterator, Mediator, Memento, Observer, State, Strategy, Template and Visitor.

title: Singleton
content:
Singleton Design Pattern is a creational pattern that ensures a class has only one instance and provides a global point of access to it.

When to use Singleton:
  • Managing shared resources: database connections, configuration settings, logging, etc.
  • Ensuring a class is instantiated only once throughout the application.

title: Builder
content: Builder is a creational design pattern that lets you construct complex objects step by step. The pattern allows you to produce different types and representations of an object using the same construction code.

title: Adapter
content:
Adapter is a structural design pattern that allows objects with incompatible interfaces to collaborate. The Adapter acts as a wrapper between two objects. It catches calls for one object and transforms them to format and interface recognizable by the second object. The adapter pattern convert the interface of a class into another interface clients expect. Adapter lets classes work together that couldn’t otherwise because of incompatible interfaces.

Adapter pattern should be used when:
  • There is an existing class and its interface does not match the one you need.
  • We need to connect systems or components that were not built to work together.

title: Facade
content: A complex interface to the client is considered as a fault in the design of a system. A Facade Pattern can help us to resolve this design problem. The Facade Pattern makes a complex interface easier to use, using a Facade class. The Facade Pattern provides a unified interface to a set of interface in a subsystem. Facade defines a higher-level interface that makes the subsystem easier to use. The Facade unifies the complex low-level interfaces of a subsystem in-order to provide a simple way to access that interface. Facade just provides a layer to the complex interfaces of the sub-system which makes it easier to use. The Facade do not encapsulate the subsystem classes or interfaces. Facade just provides a simplified interface to their functionality.

title: Proxy
content: Proxy pattern allows us to create proxy or agent of an real object.

title: Mediator
content:
The Mediator Design Pattern simplifies communication between multiple objects in a system by centralizing their interactions through a mediator. Instead of objects interacting directly, they communicate via a mediator, reducing dependencies and making the system easier to manage. The mediator pattern focuses on providing a mediator between objects for communication and helps in implementing loose coupling between objects.

Real-Life Analogy of the Mediator Design Pattern:
Colleagues (Students): Each student in the class is like a colleague. They have their tasks but need to work together on a project.
Mediator (Teacher): The teacher is like the mediator. Instead of students talking directly to each other, they talk to the teacher.

Communication Flow:
If a student needs information from another, they tell the teacher.
The teacher figures out how to get the needed information from the other students.
The teacher then relays the information back to the requesting student.
Students communicate indirectly through the teacher, keeping things organized and preventing chaos in the classroom.

title: Visitor
content: Visitor pattern allows to add operations to an existing object without altering existing object. Visitor pattern follows open-closed principles.
